NavController is NOT passed into the composable.

round1_fixes_verified.evidence: Task 10 lines 22, 54, 63, 109, 112-113 all correctly attribute FQN plumbing to SanctumApplication.onCreate() + DefaultDownloadRepository per Decision T6. Task 10 explicitly states it does NOT touch this — the responsibility is Task 7's SanctumApplication.

round1_fixes_verified.issue: TAC-1 lint grep used HTML class='error' pattern against unstable HTML output
round1_fixes_verified.evidence: Task 14 TAC-1 (lines 27-28, 79-80, 130-132) now consistently uses XML lint report (lint-results-debug.xml) with grep -c 'severity="Error"'. Explanatory comment added on line 132 explains the rationale for XML over HTML.

findings.issue: Implementation hints section (line 222) still says 'Читать lint-отчёт как plain HTML: посчитать count("<issue severity=\"Error\"") или grep -c "class=\"error\""'. This directly contradicts the updated TAC-1 approach (which now correctly uses XML reports with severity="Error" attribute). An executor following this hint would use the HTML approach and may get wrong results — HTML lint report format is not guaranteed across AGP versions and class="error" matching is fragile.
findings.fix: Update Implementation hints line 222 to: 'Читать lint-отчёт как XML: grep -c \'severity="Error"\' app/build/reports/lint-results-debug.xml — XML-формат стабильнее HTML (атрибут severity на элементе <issue>). HTML-вариант с class="error" нестабилен.'

findings.issue: qa-report.json schema (lines 171-172) stores lint report paths as .html: 'app/build/reports/lint-results-debug.html' and 'core-runtime/build/reports/lint-results-debug.html'. After the TAC-1 fix, the actual verification uses lint-results-debug.xml files. An executor faithfully recording the report will write HTML paths into the qa-report.json even though the actual checked files are XML — creating a misleading audit trail.
findings.fix: Update qa-report.json schema fields to: 'app_lint_report': 'app/build/reports/lint-results-debug.xml' and 'core_runtime_lint_report': 'core-runtime/build/reports/lint-results-debug.xml'.

findings.issue: APK size command 'stat -c %s <path>' (Details §APK metadata, line 158) is still present. This was flagged as minor in batch5-r1 and not yet fixed. stat -c %s is Linux-only syntax and unreliable in git-bash on Windows 11 (the project's confirmed OS per env). The hint in the same line correctly lists wc -c as Windows alternative, but the primary command listed first is the Linux one.
findings.fix: Reorder to show Windows-safe command first: 'Size: wc -c < <path> (git-bash, Windows-safe); or stat -c %s <path> (Linux/WSL).' Matches the existing hint that already lists wc -c as the alternative.
